rationale: Y matches Heart Rhythm's structured all-caps IMRaD format, frames the gap more precisely by naming both the thromboinflammatory and RFA-comparison questions, and adds a clinically actionable conclusion statement without overclaiming beyond the study design.
loser_weakness: X lacked the journal-formatted section headers consistent with Heart Rhythm's house style and ended with an abrupt conclusion that stopped short of translating findings into a clinical monitoring implication.
